title: Squash Commits Before Merge
description:
## Tidy the History

The repository at **`/root/squashrepo`** (branch `main`) has **4 commits**: an initial commit plus 3 noisy work-in-progress commits on top.

**Your task:** collapse the **last 3 commits into a single commit**, leaving the initial commit intact — so the branch ends up with exactly **2 commits**.

> 💡 No interactive editor is available here. Use the non-interactive trick: `git reset --soft HEAD~3` (moves the branch back but keeps all changes staged), then make one new `git commit`.

hints.title: Soft reset, then recommit
hints.body: reset --soft rewinds the branch pointer but leaves the combined changes staged in the index, so a single commit squashes them.
hints.command: cd /root/squashrepo && git reset --soft HEAD~3 && git commit -m 'squashed feature work'

setup_commands.command: cd /root/squashrepo && echo init > a.txt && git add a.txt && git commit -m initial
cd /root/squashrepo && echo 1 >> a.txt && git commit -am wip1 && echo 2 >> a.txt && git commit -am wip2 && echo 3 >> a.txt && git commit -am wip3

validation.description: Checks that main has exactly 2 commits after squashing.
